commentary: Alzarri Joseph to Assad Vala, Caught by Roston Chase!! Stunner! Chase has plucked off a blinder. Short of length delivery angled across the left hander, Vala saw the width and slashed hard, the ball flies towards backward point and Chase moves to his left to take it with both hands. That was struck hard and the ball was traveling but Chase made it look easy. He didn't even celebrate, just got up and rolled the ball to the umpire. West Indies end the powerplay with another wicket.

commentary_short: Alei Nao to Charles, Lbw!! A dream start for Alei Nao. Gets a wicket off his first ball. Charles has to go for a golden
commentary: Alei Nao to Charles, Lbw!! A dream start for Alei Nao. Gets a wicket off his first ball. Charles has to go for a golden duck. The nipbacker on the stumps, Charles' feet does not move much, the bat gets stuck and it does not prevent the ball from rapping the pad. Struck above the kneeroll, and the only question was if it would have gone over the stumps. No. It is clipping and the original decision stands. PNG have the early wicket they needed. Charles goes but does not waste a review.

commentary: John Kariko to Pooran, Caught by Tony Ura!! Kariko leaps in delight and punches the air as he gets the big wicket of Pooran. He holes out at long-on looking to clear the boundary. Tossed up on off, Pooran dances down looking to go straight but perhaps gets too close to the pitch of it, failing to get under the ball. His lofted shot only goes as far as Ura at long-on who makes no mistake.

commentary: Assad Vala to Brandon King, Caught by Lega Siaka!! PNG are fighting back with their spinners. It's captain Assad Vala this time, and he gets rid of Brandon King who was looking good out there. Tossed up on middle, slow through the air and probably deceives King in flight as he goes for the slog-sweep. He's early through the shot with the bottom hand also coming off. Miscues it off the toe-end and offers a simple catch to deep midwicket.

commentary: Assad Vala to Rutherford, Caught by Kiplin Dorigta!! Another under-edge, and this time it's a chance for the keeper who takes a brilliant catch, albeit with some fortune. Oh West Indies are in trouble here. Slightly shorter than a length outside off, Rutherford looks to pull but it doesn't bounce as much as he expects. Drags it off an under-edge, goes on to hit the keeper's thigh and he somehow latches onto the rebound. PNG are absolutely pumped.

commentary: Erasmus to Mohammad Nadeem, Lbw!! Oman don't have any reviews left and a puzzled Nadeem has to depart! Tossed up on a fuller length on middle and leg, spinning in, Mohammad Nadeem looks to flick it across but plays down the wrong line to get struck in front. The umpire took his time but ruled it out eventually. The only thing that could have saved him is if it would have spun down leg. The replay shows that it would have gone on to hit the leg-stump.

Flat delivery that bounces extra outside off. Erasmus shuffles in and tries to pull it in front of square. Wasn't the best option against the spinner and didn't choose the best of the ground for that shot either. Pulled without any real power and he ends up hitting it down the throat of wide long-on.

commentary: Bilal Khan to Wiese, , what a crucial six from David Wiese!! That hung in the air for an eternity and so did Namibia's hopes with that, but ends up going the distance. A slower length ball onto off. Wiese stays deep, settles under that one and smokes it across the line. More height that distance but targetted the shorter boundary and just over Aayan Khan at wide long-on who tried his best to salvage the ball from going over

commentary: Mehran Khan to Wiese, 2 runs, superb yorker, Wiese stays very deep in his crease and drills it powerfully down the ground. So straight that it hits the non striker stumps and deflects towards long-off.

commentary_short: Mehran Khan to Wiese, byes, 1 run, A length ball that bounces steeply outside off, Wiese tried to punch it away but the
commentary: Mehran Khan to Wiese, byes, 1 run, A length ball that bounces steeply outside off, Wiese tried to punch it away but the ball goes over his attempted shot. Difficult for the keeper to grab up to the stumps, and the ball lobs off his gloves to his left. The batters scamper for a bye and Wiese wanted to take a second run but Kruger wasn't aware of the situation. Bowler Mehran also didn't notice that Wiese was trying to sneak an extra run there, and had to be reminded by Maqsood to just whip the non striker's bails and dead the ball. We play on...

commentary: Nortje to Kamindu Mendis, Caught by Reeza Hendricks!! Nortje, like Baartman, strikes in his first over as well. This is a good pick-up shot from Kamindu Mendis, but the square boundaries here are quite long. Full ball on the pads, he whips it in the air, times it well but doesn't get enough to clear the boundary. Actually, it's taken well inside the ropes by Reeza.

commentary_short: Juma Miyagi to Lega Siaka, Lega Siaka Run Out!! 1 run completed. What happened there? Lega Siaka was looking for a secon
commentary: Juma Miyagi to Lega Siaka, Lega Siaka Run Out!! 1 run completed. What happened there? Lega Siaka was looking for a second run, was always risky and looks like he's short - he doesn't look all that confident and he's yards short of his ground. Brain fade moment and PNG have lost another wicket. Miyagi shortens the length outside off, Hiri steered it backward of square on the off-side, there was never a second run there, but Hiri took on the fielder's arms - the throw is right besides the stumps and the keeper does the rest. The zing bails light up with Hiri putting in a futile dive.

Nipped back in and beat Simon Ssesazi's defensive push, onto ball-tracking: It's pitched in line and it's hitting the top of middle and off - three reds and Simon Ssesazi is OUT. Uganda have lost three on the bounce. Just pitched on leg, but that was good enough, held its line and would;'ve been crashing into the stumps.

commentary_short: Stoinis to Mehran Khan, Caught by Tim David at long-on!! Tim David cool as cucumber there. We had a great angle from sid
commentary: Stoinis to Mehran Khan, Caught by Tim David at long-on!! Tim David cool as cucumber there. We had a great angle from side on close in. He had run back and to the right, had to take it above his shoulders facing the stands. He not only took it well but brilliantly controlled the momentum of his run and very mindfully released the ball with zero fuss before crossing the rope and calmly came into play and completed the catch again. Mehran Khan tried to get another maximum to this full delivery, had too much height on it than distance. End of a decent hand from Mehran.

commentary: Netravalkar to Rizwan, Caught by Steven Taylor!! Wow! That is a stunner from Taylor and the whole USA team run up to him. The crowd are delighted with that piece of fielding. Good length ball and it swings away late, Rizwan is rooted to the crease and is squared up because of the movement, gets an outside edge and the ball was dying on the lone slip fielder, Taylor dives to his right and scoops it with one hand before it hits the ground. These are the catches that need to stick and luckily for the USA it has. Superb start!

commentary: Nosthush Kenjige to Usman Khan, Caught by Nitish Kumar!! Well, well! Two early wickets and Pakistan are in trouble. "This is not his go-to shot. He likes to cut and sweep," informs Rameez Raja on air. Usman shimmies down, does not get to the pitch of the ball but still goes through with the loft, miscues it towards long-off, Nitish runs forward to his left and takes it calmly. Kenjige justifying his captain's call of giving him the new ball and another over.

commentary: Ali Khan to Babar Azam, leg byes, 1 run, where was Babar going? Good length ball and nips in, Babar is beaten on the flick and struck high on the pad. Ali Khan went up for the LBW appeal but it was high and hence umpire was not interested. Babar, meanwhile, wanted a single and was halfway down without seeing where the ball went and Fakhar did not respond, the fielder from backward point has a shy and fails to hit the stumps at the striker's end. The pair eventually get the single as there was no one backing up

commentary_short: Ali Khan to Fakhar Zaman, Caught by Steven Taylor!! It gets better for USA. Ali Khan is off on a celebratory run. He has
commentary: Ali Khan to Fakhar Zaman, Caught by Steven Taylor!! It gets better for USA. Ali Khan is off on a celebratory run. He has picked up a massive wicket here. This was premeditated by Fakhar and he pays the price. Back of a length delivery and at the stumps, Fakhar shuffled inside the line to play the lap and gets hit on the glove, the ball lobs to short fine and it is a simpler catch for Taylor this time. USA are cock-a-hoop.

commentary: Nosthush Kenjige to Shadab Khan, Caught by Netravalkar!! The pause in play does the trick for USA. The parnership has been broken and Kenjige is elated. Not sure what the issue was with Shadab that he needed the physio to have a look. Nonetheless, this was a harmless delivery sliding down leg, Shadab looks to sweep it over short fine and does not get the elevation, Netravalkar takes a good catch over his head.

commentary: Nosthush Kenjige to Azam Khan, Lbw!! Azam gone for a duck. Pakistan are back in trouble. This game keeps twisting and turning. Just when Pakistan had started to get the momentum USA have struck back with two wickets. This was a straight ball and Azam played all round it, he stayed rooted to the crease and missed the nudge to the leg-side, struck right in front of the stumps and ball-tracking showed it to be just hitting leg-stump.

commentary: Haris Rauf to Gous, Bowled!! That's a great delivery and Pakistan finally break the partnership. A good length delivery on off-stump, went slightly wide of the crease to angle it in and Gous was caught in his crease, he was looking to run it down to third man but he missed it completely, it went past the inside edge and hit the top of off-stump. A good knock from Gous nonetheless. Pakistan still very much on the backfoot despite the wicket.

commentary: Amir to Monank Patel, Caught by Rizwan!! This is exactly what Pakistan needed! A brilliant over from Rauf built up the pressure and now Amir gets rid of the other set batter as well. Game thrown wide open once more! A length delivery slanting across outside off, Monank Patel skipped down the track and made some room looking to slap it over extra cover, lost his shape a little as he went very hard at it. Just got the faintest of edges and Rizwan tumbled to his right before completing the catch.

commentary: Erasmus to McMullen, Stumped!! That's very sloppy from McMullen. Misses the drive to a length delivery, Zane Green fumbles and the ball rolls close to the stumps, Zane Green spots that McMullen is dozy and whips the bails off. McMullen's back foot was outside the crease and in the air, the heel did not get back in. He had all the time in the world to get back, but he was holding the pose and did not know where the heel was, it was in the air and not down on the ground. Poor cricket that.

commentary_short: Hasaranga to Towhid Hridoy, , hat-trick of sixes! This time over wide long-off. Hasaranga again keeps it so full but tri
commentary: Hasaranga to Towhid Hridoy, , hat-trick of sixes! This time over wide long-off. Hasaranga again keeps it so full but tries bowling wide of off but doesn't work as Hridoy launches the drive over long-off. Bangladesh are suddenly cruising now

commentary_short: Hasaranga to Towhid Hridoy, Lbw!! Hasaranga gets his revenge but Hridoy might have just put the game in Bangladesh's bag
commentary: Hasaranga to Towhid Hridoy, Lbw!! Hasaranga gets his revenge but Hridoy might have just put the game in Bangladesh's bag with those three sixes. Bold of Hasaranga to keep it full again. Was the leggie this time, Hridoy did read it, so he went for another slog-sweep, was probably done for extra pace and it skidded on. Impact in line with the stumps. Celebreappeal from Hasaranga but umpire Paul Reiffel said no. Instant review even before the timer came on and he was so confident that was out. It was plumb indeed as ball-tracker said smashing middle of middle stump. Maybe the length was too full for that shot but Hridoy's done a fabulous job.

commentary_short: Pathirana to Shakib, Caught by Theekshana!! Ohh Maheesh Theekshana has taken an absolute blinder. We've seen him mess up
commentary: Pathirana to Shakib, Caught by Theekshana!! Ohh Maheesh Theekshana has taken an absolute blinder. We've seen him mess up so many times on the field but look at him keep his eyes on this ball that was dying on him and time his jump forward to perfection and the technique to get his hands under the ball before it went to the ground was brilliant. Literally wanted to scoop the ball and controlled it nicely into the palms. Is this going to inspire the Sri Lankans? Sure Pathirana is pumped. Was a short delivery outside off stump, Shakib tried to guide it uppishly to the third man fence but Sri Lanka had their man straighter and got the reward.

commentary: N Thushara to Rishad Hossain, Bowled!! That is wild from Rishad Hossain. There was no need to try a big slog at this stage when senior pro Mahmudullah is looking to play this over from Thushara. Rishad not only went for the big slog, but also exposed the stumps and he was a sitting duck with his off-speed delivery that forced sucked him early into the shot.

commentary: Nortje to Edwards, Edwards Run Out!! Just after playing a fantastic shot, Edwards throws his wicket away by taking a suicidal run-out. South African bowlers dominating the game just like they did against Sri Lanka.

commentary: Ottneil Baartman to van Beek, Caught by Nortje!! Ottneil Baartman finishes with a 4-fer! van Beek makes room and Ottneil Baartman bowls a well-executed slower one. van Beek looks to go over covers but completely mistimes it. Nortje at extra cover moves back and takes a well-judged catch.

commentary_short: Kingma to Reeza Hendricks, de Kock Run Out!! Oh dear, de Kock has been run out after a terrible mix-up. Full ball strayi
commentary: Kingma to Reeza Hendricks, de Kock Run Out!! Oh dear, de Kock has been run out after a terrible mix-up. Full ball straying down leg, Hendricks misses and it goes off his pad to short fine. There's plenty of indecision on his part about going for the single. He comes down, then turns back, then goes again before eventually deciding to abort. Meanwhile, de Kock is more than halfway down the pitch and there's no way back for him as van Meekeren returns the throw at the bowler's end.

commentary: Kingma to Markram, no run, yorker outside off, gentle outswinger, Markram looks to squeeze it away but it goes through to the keeper. Edwards is excited about a possible edge and Netherlands have gone for a . Replay shows it's a bump ball as Markram jammed it off the toe-end into the pitch first

commentary_short: Kingma to Markram, Caught by Edwards!! Netherlands are celebrating while the umpires get together to confirm whether the
commentary: Kingma to Markram, Caught by Edwards!! Netherlands are celebrating while the umpires get together to confirm whether the catch is taken cleanly. They've sent it upstairs. Markram is shaking his head. Length ball down leg, he looks to clip it away but it nips in and he gets a faint inside edge. Edwards dives across to the left and it's hard to say for sure if the ball has touched the ground or not. Third umpire is convinced that the fingers are underneath the ball. Oh we might have a game on our hands here.

commentary: Bas de Leede to Tristan Stubbs, Caught by van Beek!! There's still life left in this match. It's not over yet. A short delivery by Bas de Leede and Tristan Stubbs pulls it towards deep mid-wicket. van Beek charges in from the boundary, settles under the skier and completes a safe catch. South Africa need 27 runs from 22 balls, not at all scoring those runs on this particular wicket. Batters have struggled here right throughout the tournament.

commentary: van Beek to Marco Jansen, Bowled!! This match keeps swinging like the pendulum of a clock! Short of a length around middle and off, the ball seams back in to beat Marco Jansen's wild swing. The middle stump has been uprooted and South Africa need 16 runs from 13 balls. Are Netherlands going to come out on top once again in this battle? They beat SA in the previous two ICC events and they might do it once again.

commentary_short: Moeen Ali to Warner, Bowled!! England needed this, Moeen needed this - the two openers were running away with the contes
commentary: Moeen Ali to Warner, Bowled!! England needed this, Moeen needed this - the two openers were running away with the contest - nicely bowled by Moeen, dragged the length back, offered no pace at all and the ball stayed devilishly low - Warner had backed away on the cut, he has to wait and wait, that forces the mistake as he looked to go hard on the cut, the ball sneaks under and crashes into the middle-stump. A superb cameo comes to an end and Warner walks off to a good round of applause from the Aussie supporters.

commentary_short: Jofra Archer to Head, Bowled!! Australia have lost both openers after a roaring start - it's the slower ball which does
commentary: Jofra Archer to Head, Bowled!! Australia have lost both openers after a roaring start - it's the slower ball which does the trick for Archer and he's chuffed to bits, rolled his fingers across the seam on a good length delivery around off, Head had backed away and was looking to go over the off-side, but he's undone by the lack of pace and the ball clatters into the middle-stump. Can England force their way back into the match?

commentary: Livingstone to Mitchell Marsh, Stumped!! Lovely delivery has got Mitchell Marsh. It is nicely tossed up outside the leg-stump, pitches and turns, beats Mitchell Marsh's inside-out loft, Buttler misses the first time but whips the bails off, Marsh did not get his back foot back in time. The partnership is broken and Livingstone has justified the decision of him being thrown the ball.

Australia need a reset after those two wickets.

Uganda have had top order issues and the free fall has happened today as well.

commentary: Bumrah to Babar Azam, Caught by Suryakumar Yadav!! This is an outstanding catch by SKY. If not for his timely dive this couldn't have been possible. But they are checking if it cleanly carried. Looked clean to the naked eye. OUT and India have their first. Back of a length working for Bumrah. This time he got a bit more bounce with some away movement. Babar was opened up in his defence and he almost guided that off the face of the bat. Actually wanted to bail out but couldn't drag his bat away. Slip was very wide, close to second slip but SKY was so alert to the dying ball and quickly dived low to grab this inches from the ground.

commentary: Hardik Pandya to Fakhar Zaman, Caught by Pant!! Fakhar outsmarted by Hardik! He advances, gets a short of length delivery at his chest and is completely cramped for room as he goes for the pull shot. It balloons off the shoulder after taking the top edge and goes behind Pant who takes a sharp catch. He wasn't far from stepping on the helmet as he pedalled back. Thankfully for India, that didn't happen as he hopped back to grab it behind his right shoulder.

commentary: Hardik Pandya to Shadab Khan, 2 runs, good running. Back of a length on leg, Shadab tucks it into the vacant midwicket region, sees the opportunity and charges back for two. Fielder had to come in a long way from the boundary as Shadab had played it quite gently

commentary: Hardik Pandya to Shadab Khan, Caught by Pant!! Hardik shrugs his shoulders in celebration as he bounces another batter out. Massive roar at Nassau County. Banged in short, follows Shadab as he backs away and gets good height on it. The ball gets big on Shadab as he goes for the pull and balloons off the top-edge. No mistake from Pant running to his left. Nerves jangling now for Pakistan.

commentary: Bumrah to Iftikhar Ahmed, Caught by Arshdeep Singh!! Another full toss on the pads for Iftikhar, and he misses out again. Swings across the line, gets a thick leading edge and it goes high in the air. There's nearly a collision between Arshdeep and Surya in the deep. But Arshdeep hangs on despite the distraction.

commentary: Arshdeep Singh to Imad Wasim, Caught by Pant!! Cracking yorker following Wasim down the leg-side. He looks to swing but misses, and it goes through to Pant. The two batters manage to nab a bye. Oh the umpire has given it out for caught behind. Did he get an inside edge on it? That's a cracking decision from umpire Illingworth as UltraEdge confirms there was an inside edge. The task getting harder and harder for Pakistan now.

commentary: Taskin Ahmed to Markram, Bowled!! South Africa in disarray early once more. A good length delivery angling into the stumps, Markram pushed through the line again and there was just enough seam movement to beat the outside edge before it went on to hit middle-stump. Superb start for Bangladesh, they've got three early wickets now and they're bowling really nicely.

commentary_short: Tanzim Hasan Sakib to Tristan Stubbs, Caught by Shakib!! This is going downhill very quickly for South Africa now as the
commentary: Tanzim Hasan Sakib to Tristan Stubbs, Caught by Shakib!! This is going downhill very quickly for South Africa now as they've lost a fourth in the powerplay. Once more it's the tacky nature of the surface that's to blame here. A back of a length delivery outside off, Tristan Stubbs stood tall and just pushed it towards cover but was through his shot a fraction too early because it stopped on him, it went low to Shakib at cover and he did really well to dive forward and hold on to this. It's been all about the bowlers here in New York and there's nothing different today.

commentary: Maharaj to Mahmudullah, Caught by Markram!! Mahmudullah doesn't want to leave. He stands stunned after seeing the South African skipper pull off an excellent boundary-flirting catch. A low full toss from Maharaj and Mahmudullah tonked it really hard straight down the ground. Markram shows excellent awareness as he runs back to his left to take a high-pressure catch well above his head. Had he missed it, that would have been game over.

commentary: Arshdeep Singh to Nitish Kumar, Caught by Siraj!! 133.2kph, short ball, Nitish Kumar moves leg-side to pull. Sounds good but he gets more height than distance. Siraj, standing a few inches in front of the boundary rope at deep square leg, times his jump perfectly after taking a couple of steps backwards. A brilliant overhead catch and he ensures that the ball stays in his hands even as he falls down. Nitish looked good, but he has to depart.

commentary: Hardik Pandya to Corey Anderson, Caught by Pant!! High, but it isn't long. Pant calls for it and so does the bowler, but the latter leaves it to the former and the Indian wicketkeeper doesn't err. 132.4kph, fuller than a length, Corey Anderson jogs down the track and in the process moves leg-side to swing it over the leg-side. The ball goes a mile up in the air off the leading edge and Pant runs towards the square leg region to complete a good catch.

commentary: Boult to Charles, Bowled!! And gone. Charles looked to thrash that through the off-side and gets a big inside edge for the ball to slam into the sticks. Huge start for the Kiwis. He backed away to give himself room but got a thick edge for it to thud into the stumps. The bounce again was on the lower side. Wicket in the first over, Boult off the mark. NZ would be elated as well.

commentary: Southee to Pooran, Caught by Conway!! The short ball has done the trick. Good take from Conway despite getting himself into a tangle. The ball was swirling and Conway chased it but despite being a little clumsy he managed to take it clean. He almost overran the ball. They are checking did it hit one of the camera wires? Did it deviate a little and that confused the keeper to overrun it? The third umpire had a look but confirmed it is fine. Southee in his comeback game has a wicket here. Pooran was looking to swing hard but has fallen prey to the short ball. I guess he was a little late on it.

The batter again seemed to be a little late on it. Not many short balls but has already yielded two wickets. Windies in huge trouble.

commentary: Southee to Powell, Caught by Conway!! Gone. Fourth one down. Length delivery at 78.5 mph. Powell looks to guide it down to third man but the ball decked in a little and went off the edge for the keeper to dive forward low and take it. Windies in deep trouble. commentary: Alzarri Joseph to Glenn Phillips, Caught by Powell!! That should be the game. Powell knows it and so do the crowd. They roar. A short of length delivery and bit of extra pace maybe, Glenn Phillips makes room to pull, but it hits the bat high and Powell is an athlete, runs in from long-on and takes the pressure catch. Made it look easy for at one moment, it looked like it would fall in front of him.

commentary: Alzarri Joseph to Southee, Caught&Bowled!! Alzarri Joseph has four. West Indies are almost home now. Southee shuffles across outside off and on the flick he gets a top-edge of sorts, Alzarri Joseph runs back and takes it well. Again looked like it would fall in space. Joseph on a hat-trick now.

commentary_short: Aryan Dutt to Litton Das, Caught by Sybrand Engelbrecht!! 98.3kph, in-drifter on a fullish length outside off, Litton Da
commentary: Aryan Dutt to Litton Das, Caught by Sybrand Engelbrecht!! 98.3kph, in-drifter on a fullish length outside off, Litton Das shuffles across and gets low to slogsweep. More height than distance but it looked like it would land safely. However, Sybrand Engelbrecht  had other ideas. Sprinted swiftly to his left from deep square leg, dived across with outstretched hands to pull off a stunning catch. Secod wicket for Aryan Dutt!

commentary: Rishad Hossain to Sybrand Engelbrecht, Caught by Tanzim Hasan Sakib!! Rishad breaks the partnership! Tossed up outside off, tempts Engelbrecht to go for a lofted shot down the ground but the ball grips and turns, taking a thick outside edge. It balloons in the air towards backward point where Tanzim somehow hangs on. Seems like the sun got in his eyes and he had to turn away, but manages to hang on.

commentary: Rishad Hossain to Bas de Leede, Stumped!! Litton appears confident about a stumping. It's a cracking legbreak from Rishad. Tossed up, drifting in on middle before gripping and turning sharply past the edge. He lures de Leede into the drive but he's completely beaten by the dip and turn. He ends up overbalancing, dragging the back foot out of the crease. And Litton makes him pay with some sharp glovework.

Oh Nepal, all the hardwork being frittered away in a moment of panic or two.

commentary_short: Ottneil Baartman to Gulsan Jha, Gulsan Jha Run Out!! A run-out shout. The third umpire will have a look. This looks very
commentary: Ottneil Baartman to Gulsan Jha, Gulsan Jha Run Out!! A run-out shout. The third umpire will have a look. This looks very close - has Jha plonked his bat in and forced a super over? Nervous look all-around -. Oh goodness, Jha looks gutted, Nepal look gutted - they've lost a match they could and they should have won. Relief in the South African camp, look at their delight, they've done a houdini act and have come from nowhere. Coming to the ball, was banged in short and just outside off - Jha was looking to run it past the keeper and missed, de Kock went for the bowler's end as Sompal Kami scampered across, but the ball hit Jha's back and deflected towards cover. Klaasen picked the ball and under-armed the flick onto the stumps at the bowler's end, Jha must've thought he had saved himself, was a tad late in plonking his bat down and he's caught short by a yard.

commentary: Maxwell to Cross, Caught by Agar!! Lucky dismissal for Maxwell. A loopy full toss from him, Cross makes some room and gives it a big baseball swing across the line. Ends up getting more height that distance, holes out to deep mid-wicket and Australia hold on to the catch this time. Glenn Maxwell would have bowled plenty of better balls and not got wickets in the past, but gets one with a rank full toss this time. That's the luck of the draw sometimes.

commentary: Safyaan Sharif to Head, Caught by Michael Jones!! OUT!! Oh how the whole of Scotland needed that wicket!! Safyaan Sharif goes round the wicket and sticks with that full, wide line of his. Head can't get fully under it on this occasion, ends up hitting it down the throat of Jones at long-off. Jones dropped Travis Head in the powerplay, and will that prove costly? Has the damage already been done?

The worst time to drop a catch. A full delivery on off, David went for the glory shot. Went for a big heave across the line and hits it a kilometer up in the sky. Sole from deep mid-wicket settles under it but can't hold on

commentary: Shaheen Afridi to Balbirnie, no run, not much movement on ball 1 and ball 2. That didn't bend in enough and hit the pads a little high. Balbirnie didn't use his feet much. The ball hit him and there was an appeal

commentary_short: Shaheen Afridi to Balbirnie, Bowled!! And cleans him up. That was a lovely length delivery pitched up, Balbirnie looked
commentary: Shaheen Afridi to Balbirnie, Bowled!! And cleans him up. That was a lovely length delivery pitched up, Balbirnie looked to drive and left a gap between pad and bat. The ball traversed through the gap and slammed into the sticks. He is gone for a golden duck and his campaign ends on a poor note. That was superb from Shaheen Afridi as he got the ball to curl in. He hasn't been able to do this much. But not today. Got it right and Pakistan have put Ireland under pressure.

commentary: Shaheen Afridi to Tucker, 2 runs, 138.4 kph, Tucker walks into the push and gets an inside edge. The ball misses the sticks and dribbles past the sticks for a brace. Tucker walked at the delivery and lucky that it missed the sticks

commentary_short: Shaheen Afridi to Tucker, Caught by Rizwan!! Fuller ball and goes away to take the edge. Pakistan have Ireland on the ma
commentary: Shaheen Afridi to Tucker, Caught by Rizwan!! Fuller ball and goes away to take the edge. Pakistan have Ireland on the mat now. They want to pin them here. That was a fuller one, nipped away, Tucker played it into the hands of the keeper by going for the drive. Not a wicket where you can do it so early as the ball is moving around. Two down in the first over.

commentary: Shaheen Afridi to Tector, no run, fuller ball, nipped in, Tector looked to poke at it and missed the line of the ball. The ball went between bat and pad and did give the impression that he nicked it. But the ball touched the flap of the pad and went into the gloves. The review was taken but Tector was safe

commentary: Amir to Stirling, Caught by Rizwan!! Length delivery, seamed away at 125.8 kph. Stirling looked to dance down and drive on the up but got a thin edge on it. The keeper did the rest. That ball, the length was pulled back and that did the trick. Little bit of extra bounce and Stirling had no chance to survive that. Easy take for the keeper. His second grab. Amir is off the mark as well like his bowling partner. Pakistan all over Ireland.

But Amir pulled his length back and has used his experience to bargain a wicket. Pakistan bowlers coming good but a little too late.

The ball went straight up and the man at square leg called for it and took it easily. Haris Rauf strikes in his first over. That was bowled at close to 84 mph and Curtis Campher should have been careful with a cross batted swipe. But he looked to aggressive and has paid the price for it.

commentary: Imad Wasim to Delany, Caught by Shadab Khan!! Imad Wasim strikes in his first over! The 44-run stand has been broken. Slower through the air, drifts in and spins away from a length with decent bounce. Delany looks to work it into the leg-side off the back foot and closes his bat-face early. Gets a leading edge and the ball goes straight to point. A soft dismissal and Delany's brave innings comes to an end. First wicket in this WC for Imad.

commentary: Rohit Paudel to Shakib, Lbw!! A length delivery drifting into leg-stump, lot of bite and turn again, Shakib went right back looking to work it into the leg-side and ended up playing all around it. The turn meant that it beat the outside edge and it went on to hit his back leg. There wasn't much hope for him on the review. No bat involved and all three reds on ball-tracker. Shakib goes and so does a review. Bangladesh in all sorts here.

commentary: Lamichhane to Tanzim Hasan Sakib, Bowled!! Bangladesh in absolute disarray here as they lose a 7th wicket. Tossed up full on off-stump but only this time it's the wrong-un, Tanzim Hasan Sakib plays for the leg-break, leaves a massive gap between bat and pad and that's exactly where the ball goes through before smashing into the stumps. Lamichhane has his first wicket of the tournament.

Rohit Paudel out cheaply, he got just a bit carried away perhaps in his joust with Tanzim.

commentary: Mustafizur to Aasif Sheikh, Caught by Shakib!! Another wicket, and it's a big one. Aasif Sheikh was the batter who got most of those runs against South Africa. He was looking good today as well but his innings has been cut short. A tad overpitched and just outside off, Sheikh goes for a cover drive but can't keep it down. Shakib at short cover almost makes a mess of it, takes the catch on a second attempt. Almost burst through his hands, lobs up off his shoulder and allows him a second chance at the catch.

commentary: Mustafizur to Kushal Malla, Caught by Shanto!! The partnership has been broken and Malla, who was just starting to open up his shoulders, is gone. Was a slower delivery full and just outside off, change of pace did the trick as Kushal Malla went for a big hit, toe ended it and it went straight up, Shanto ran a few paces back from mid-off and held on safely. Big wicket for Bangladesh.

commentary: Mustafizur to Dipendra Singh, Caught by Litton Das!! It does not get much better. That's a fantastic over, another slower delivery on off-stump, cuts away off the deck, Dipendra Singh took a step down the wicket and opened the face of the bat looking to run it down to third man, he wanted to keep the strike but all he could manage was a faint outside edge. Nepal just made things really hard for themselves.

commentary: Shakib to Abinash Bohara, Lbw!! Length delivery on middle-leg, Bohara went right across his stumps looking to work it into the leg-side, he went too far across and missed, hit him on the thigh pad but he was right back in his crease too. No bat involved and all three reds on ball-tracker.

commentary: Kingma to Nissanka, Caught by Sybrand Engelbrecht!! Nissanka falls for a duck and it's a fine diving catch by Engelbrecth, at third man.  Hew's been everywhere for Netherlands in this tournament - fuller length delivery and shaping away from wide of off, Nissanka saw the width and threw his hands on the drive, sliced it high off the outside edge - Engelbrecth was stationed at wide third man, ran in and was helped by the wind which made the ball hang in the air forever, but he still had to put in a dive forward and he then hung on to a good low catch.

commentary: Aryan Dutt to Kusal Mendis, Caught by Sybrand Engelbrecht!! Mendis has holed out to deep mid-wicket, it's Engelbrecth once again - dragged short by Dutt, it's right there for the pull, but Mendis is hitting it to the longest boundary and he's hitting it against the wind as well. The ball hung in the air and Engelbrecth is right under it when it comes down. A handy knock from Mendis, but he'd be gutted at not having gone deep.

commentary: Tim Pringle to Dhananjaya, Caught by Bas de Leede!! A big slog-sweep, a bigger top-edge and DDS has holed out to deep mid-wicket. Nicely bowled by Pringle, slowed down the pace, got some extra bounce and forced a miscue as DDS looked to clear the mid-wicket boundary, he's playing against the wind and he needs to get it 100% spot-on, but he only manages a massive top-edge and de Leede reverse cusps it with ease.

commentary: Shanaka to Vikramjit Singh, Caught by Kamindu Mendis!! The catch is taken on the second attempt with a lot of good fortune. Banged in short, Vikramjit goes for the pull and miscues it off the top-edge as it climbs on him. Kamindu runs to his left from deep midwicket and gets to it but it sneaks through his hands. However, luckily, it falls right on the knees and pops up again, giving him a second chance and he latches onto it.

commentary: Hasaranga to van Beek, van Beek Run Out!! The innings is falling apart quickly for Netherlands. Van Beek gone first ball. commentary_short: Harmeet Singh to de Kock, Caught by S Jahangir at deep mid-wicket!! They have been feeding full tosses to Quinton and on
commentary: Harmeet Singh to de Kock, Caught by S Jahangir at deep mid-wicket!! They have been feeding full tosses to Quinton and one has eventually bought them a wicket and it's the big fish too. Hang on, they are checking the height on that one. "Satisfied, that's a fair delivery" says third ump Joel Wilson. QdK tried to smash that hit-me ball for six but it took a well judged catch very close to the rope to send him back. Jahangir had to control his momentum and ensure he didn't step on the rope and he was great. Quinton walks back after a great hand.

commentary_short: Harmeet Singh to Miller, Caught&Bowled!! Oh he's chipped it back to the bowler. Harmeet Singh is suddenly on a hat-trick
commentary: Harmeet Singh to Miller, Caught&Bowled!! Oh he's chipped it back to the bowler. Harmeet Singh is suddenly on a hat-trick. South Africa pushed Miller over Stubbs to keep a left-hander at the crease since Quinton got out but Miller has thrown it away. Nothing great about this ball though. Regulation flighted delivery, probably stopped on the batter and it's a straight chip for an easy return catch. Can USA get back into this game with this momentum?

commentary_short: Livingstone to Powell, Caught by Mark Wood!! Livingstone has won the battle of the big hitters, but it has come at some
commentary: Livingstone to Powell, Caught by Mark Wood!! Livingstone has won the battle of the big hitters, but it has come at some cost - a flooaty leg-break outside off, Powell was shaping up for another mighty slog, but the ball dipped on him and he lost his poise completely, slugs across the line, gets a thick outside edge which lobbed towards short third man - Wood runs in, dives forward and gets both hands underneath the ball, it's a fair catch and the 3rd umpire confirms the same. Powell falls after a handy cameo.

commentary_short: Roston Chase to Buttler, Lbw!! Buttler has been trapped in front but he opts for the review! Buttler used his feet but g
commentary: Roston Chase to Buttler, Lbw!! Buttler has been trapped in front but he opts for the review! Buttler used his feet but got himself into a tangle. Roston Chase fired in a full-toss and Buttler played all around to it get struck on the pads. Roston Chase appealed vociferously and up went the umpire's finger. Time for the replays. UltraEdge shows clear daylight between bat and ball. Over to ball-tracking and it returns Is this the moment for WI to surge a fightback?

commentary: Adil Rashid to de Kock, no run, England are celebrating but de Kock isn't entirely convinced about the catch being clean. He's hanging around. . Oh this is tight. It's hard to say conclusively either way. But the third umpire has now found an angle which convinces him that the ball is on the ground. Massive moment in the game as de Kock gets a repreive. Tossed up outside leg, he sweeps in the air and it would've been a comfortable catch for Wood if he had just taken a couple of steps forward. But he doesn't move, turns it into a very low catch and the ball touches the ground according to the third umpire. Wood is having a chat with the umpires, claiming that his fingers were underneath. Doesn't matter now

commentary: Jofra Archer to de Kock, Caught by Buttler!! Oh superb one-handed grab from Buttler! The tide has turned in these last few overs and England are firmly in the ascendancy now with the wicket of de Kock. After taking some punishment earlier, Archer gets his man. Slower short ball outside off, de Kock looks to cut but the ball gets big on him, taking a healthy edge that balloons to the left of the keeper. Buttler dives across full stretch and plucks it brilliantly.

commentary_short: Mark Wood to Klaasen, , Klaasen Run Out!! !! You absolute beauty. He has done something similar against Steve Smith in t
commentary: Mark Wood to Klaasen, , Klaasen Run Out!! !! You absolute beauty. He has done something similar against Steve Smith in the semi final of the 2019 World Cup and now Klaasen is at the receiving end. It was a poor delivery from Mark Wood and to make matters worse, the England skipper didn't gather the ball cleanly either. Short and sprayed down leg, Klaasen allows it to sail over and Buttler fumbles while diving across to his left. The ball, however, deflects a few yards to his left allowing the skipper to take a sharp aim at the non-striker's end. Klaasen was a bit casual and didn't expect the ball to hit the stumps direct. Too good.

commentary: Maharaj to Buttler, Caught by Klaasen!! The first time Buttler tries to play across the line and against the turn he perishes. Smart of Maharaj to keep it within the stumps and force a leg-side hit when Buttler was so keen on reverse-sweeping him to the off-side. Buttler went for the slog-sweep, got it on the outer half of the bat and it flew straight to deep mid-wicket. Could have played him out but decided to attack and perished. This is getting interesting. England three down.

commentary: Rabada to Livingstone, Caught by Tristan Stubbs!! That is the seventh full toss in the last 8 balls that South Africa have bowled and it's bought them a wicket. Maybe the full toss is a wicket-taking ball, who knows! And Rabada is roaring after that wicket. He's got lucky here with Livingstone mishitting and the wind probably resisted the ball too. Taken at deep backward square leg. Will the Proteas use this opening to pull off a miracle? They surely need a miracle to win this game.

commentary: Nortje to Harry Brook, Caught by Markram!! In the airrrr, Markram has taken a brilliant catch running back from mid-off. Is this the game now for South Africa? Full and in the slot from Nortje, Brook only tried to chip. Was it the slower ball that deceived him? Definitely didn't go through with the shot. Still was clearing the infield but Markram had his eyes set on the ball running back and looking over his right shoulder. Stretched full length and took completed the catch. If this wasn't pace off, Brook will be livid for missing out on a slot ball.

commentary_short: Motie to Nitish Kumar, Lbw!! 86.4kph, tossed up ball, drifts in and dips on the batter. Nitish Kumar looks to play a pre
commentary: Motie to Nitish Kumar, Lbw!! 86.4kph, tossed up ball, drifts in and dips on the batter. Nitish Kumar looks to play a premeditated reverse-sweep and misses it to get hit on the pad adjacent to the sticks. He's been given out lbw but he reviews after a chat with his batting partner. UltraEdge confirms no bat. Over to ball-tracking and it's three reds. Allahudien Paleker's call stays. USA lose a review and the impressive Nitish Kumar has to depart. Much-needed wicket for West Indies and Motie has broken the 48-run stand in his first over.

commentary_short: Roston Chase to Corey Anderson, Lbw!! Roston Chase has got another one and it's the dangerous Corey Anderson this time.
commentary: Roston Chase to Corey Anderson, Lbw!! Roston Chase has got another one and it's the dangerous Corey Anderson this time. A length delivery on middle, darted in a touch quicker and Corey Anderson went right back looking to work it into the leg-side, he missed and was struck on the back pad, couldn't have been more plumb in front. Easy decision for the umpire and Anderson, after considering the review for a second, thinks the better of it and walks off. USA in a bit of strife here.

commentary_short: Roston Chase to Harmeet Singh, Caught by Charles!! Lovely bowling this from Roston Chase, tossed up full and just outsid
commentary: Roston Chase to Harmeet Singh, Caught by Charles!! Lovely bowling this from Roston Chase, tossed up full and just outside off, slowed it up nicely and Harmeet Singh was through his shot far too early as he leant in looking to work it into the leg-side, there was just enough turn for it to catch the leading edge as he closed the face of the bat and it lobbed up to Charles at backward point. Simplest of catches and it's just gone from bad to worse for USA. What's more, Chase will be on a hat-trick!

commentary: Harmeet Singh to Charles, Caught by Milind Kumar!! Holes out!! Tossed up slightly and at the stumps, Charles can't resist from having a go at this one. Plays a big slog sweep and connects pretty well, but Milind Kumar at deep mid-wicket takes a solid catch hugging the boundary ropes. Judges it well and ensures his feet were well inside the rope. Something to celebrate for USA.

commentary: Tanzim Hasan Sakib to Kohli, Bowled!! Tanzim Hasan Sakib gets the big fish. He has been their best bowler this tournament and he's managed to remove one of the best in the business. Perhaps expected Kohli to force the pace and shortened his length while keep it straight. Kohli wanted to go down the ground, but was duped by the length and lack of pace. The ball curls in late to hit the middle of middle.

commentary_short: Tanzim Hasan Sakib to Suryakumar Yadav, Caught by Litton Das!! SIX AND OUT. Bangladesh are making a fine comeback here.
commentary: Tanzim Hasan Sakib to Suryakumar Yadav, Caught by Litton Das!! SIX AND OUT. Bangladesh are making a fine comeback here. Extra bounce on this length delivery outside off and SKY wasn't quite sure if he wanted to defend or dab. Tried to open the bat face really late and ended up gloving it to the right of the keeper. There wasn't a strong appeal from the bowler, but SKY knew he was a goner and started walking quite instantly.

commentary: Rishad Hossain to Pant, Caught by Tanzim Hasan Sakib!! Rishad Hossain gets his man, finally. He goes fuller and faster just outside off, Pant opted for the reverse sweep but ended up dragging it off the thick inside half straight to the fielder at short third. A bit of extra bounce as well on that delivery which might have messed up with the timing of the shot.

commentary: Rishad Hossain to Shivam Dube, Bowled!! Cleaned up! Rishad has the last laugh. He looks so cool and calm under pressure. He knew Dube was coming after him and just kept it simple - full and straight, Dube misses the slog sweep and the ball crashes into the stumps. This was bowled quicker and much fuller which did not allow Dube to get under the big shot.

commentary: Hardik Pandya to Litton Das, Caught by Suryakumar Yadav!! Hardik with the bat. Hardik with the ball. Hardik doing it all. He provides India the first breakthrough. Litton would be livid with himself. He had just struck a six and tries to hit another, walks across to access the leg-side field but Hardik smartly takes pace off, Litton cannot control the pull and hits it over square leg, SKY runs in and takes a good catch. Never easy with these catches with the wind blowing strongly.

commentary_short: Jordan to Nosthush Kenjige, Lbw!! That's plumb! 141.3kph, full, quick and on the target, a shuffling Kenjige misses the
commentary: Jordan to Nosthush Kenjige, Lbw!! That's plumb! 141.3kph, full, quick and on the target, a shuffling Kenjige misses the flick to get pinned on the front pad. He's struck low on the front leg adjacent to the sticks and ball-tracking shows it to be crashing into the middle-stump! USA had two reviews left and hence they sent it upstairs. Kenjige even had a smile on his face when he signalled T. Everyone knew that it was plumb.

commentary: Maharaj to Powell, Stumped!! Beautifully bowled! Powell is completely deceived in the flight and dip. It's tossed up on off, he's drawn forward to drive but it dips and turns past the edge. He's not balanced on the shot, and the back foot is dragged out, giving de Kock an opportunity for a stumping and he takes it. Square leg umpire doesn't bother going upstairs.

commentary_short: Shamsi to Rutherford, Caught by Markram!! Edged and caught! The 3rd umpire is having a look, but Markram seems very conf
commentary: Shamsi to Rutherford, Caught by Markram!! Edged and caught! The 3rd umpire is having a look, but Markram seems very confident and Rutherford was happy to go as well, , can't get easier then that and the 3rd umpire does the formality of sending Rutherford back. Lovely bowling from Shamsi - drifted in, dragged Rutherford forward and then spun just enough to catch the outside edge on the push, the ball was dipping low, but Markram leans forward, gets both hands and completes a fine catch. Three wickets in no time and West Indies are in some trouble now.

commentary: Shamsi to Roston Chase, Caught by Rabada!! This doesn't help, this is a poor option from Roston Chase and he's thrown his wicket away. Was nicely tossed up on middle and his eyes lit up on the slog-sweep, but he's undone by the dip, gets a big top-edge towards long-on and Rabada settles under the skier. He hasn't been needed with the ball so far tonight, but he's contributed with a catch.

commentary: Rabada to Akeal Hosein, Russell Run Out!! A direct hit at the striker's end - South Africa think they've got Russell, he doesn't look confident either. Huge moment in the match and he's just short - short by an inch - . That's a damaging blow, South Africa know it, so does Russell, he didn't pit his head up as soon as the zing bails lit up - was dabbed straight to the fielder at short third, there was probably no run there, but Russell was desperate for strike, it's his call and a fatal call from the West Indies point of view - Nortje takes his time and makes a direct hit to find his man short..

commentary: Russell to Reeza Hendricks, Caught by Pooran!! A loud shout for , there was a noise, but was it off the pad? West Indies have , short and angling down leg, Reeza Hendricks was looking to flick and there's a deviation, onto UltraEdge and it's indeed off the bat - a strangle down leg and . The perfect start from Russell, got lucky, but he'll not care - on a length and going down leg, Hendricks was looking to help it on its way, a thin edge and Pooran does the rest.

commentary: Russell to de Kock, Caught by Rutherford!! Caught at deep backward square leg - Russell has two and the crowd has come to life at North South. Banged in short and de Kock is rushed for pace, hit high on the bat and he has no control over the pull, lobbed in the air and behind square on the on-side, Rutherford runs across, settles under the skier and takes the catch with his hands pointing down. Now, now - the horrors of the past can come back quickly into South Africa's minds.

commentary: Alzarri Joseph to Markram, Caught by Mayers!! Needless! Markram tries to take on Joseph first ball and perishes in the process. The partnership was building nicely and the South African skipper has thrown it away. This was in the slot alright but Markram does not get the loft anywhere close to the middle, miscues it straight up and Mayers take a couple of steps back near mid-off before hanging on. West Indies needed that wicket after an expensive previous over.

commentary: Alzarri Joseph to Klaasen, Caught by Pooran!! That's a snorter! This has to be the plan to Klaasen in T20s - fast and short. Joseph has done just that and kept West Indies in the hunt. This was a well-directed short ball, Klaasen was ducking and trying to fend at the same time, his eyes were off the ball and he was struck on the glove, the ball flew over Pooran but he ran back, jumped up and took a brilliant catch.

commentary: Roston Chase to Tristan Stubbs, Caught by Mayers!! This is a massive wicket. The set batter is gone. What was Stubbs doing? commentary_short: Roston Chase to Maharaj, Caught by Alzarri Joseph!! Chase has turned the game on its head. Three wickets in three overs
commentary: Roston Chase to Maharaj, Caught by Alzarri Joseph!! Chase has turned the game on its head. Three wickets in three overs and South Africa look worried. Maharaj decides to take on Chase and perishes in the process. He shimmies down the track and does not get to the pitch, still goes through with the big shot and mistimes it to long-off, Joseph makes no mistake. Now a left-hander comes in and it is a favourable match-up for Chase.

commentary: Stoinis to Shivam Dube, Caught by Warner!! Holes out!! Stoinis goes full on the boots, difficult for any batter to do much about these balls. commentary_short: Kuldeep Yadav to Mitchell Marsh, Caught by Axar!! Wow. Hire a Lexicographer to describe that. Third time not so lucky fo
commentary: Kuldeep Yadav to Mitchell Marsh, Caught by Axar!! Wow. Hire a Lexicographer to describe that. Third time not so lucky for Mr. Marsh. This catch reminds me of the catch taken by Adam Bacher in 1997 off Sachin Tendulkar at Newlands. The ball was going at the pace of knots. A length ball onto the pads, pulled away hard behind square. Axar was a little late on it as well but he plucked a hand out and it stuck. It was his non dominant right hand as well. India had dropped Marsh twice and it takes a stunner to get rid of him. India will look to come back into the game with this take. The catch was more special because he grabbed it off his right hand despite being a southpaw.

commentary: Kuldeep Yadav to Maxwell, Bowled!! Big blow of the Big Show!! Walks down the wicket for a big slog across the line. Kuldeep tempts the batter with a loopy full delivery. Maxwell swings through thin air and misses, and the ball rattles his stumps. A bit of a brain fade from Maxwell, walked out too early and showed his cards too early. The bowler knew he was going to big for a big shot and adjusted accordingly with the googly. Kuldeep continues to win the battle against Maxi, but will India will the battle today?

commentary: Jordan to Hardik Pandya, Caught by Sam Curran!! Was the slower delivery by Jordan, an off-break and Hardik smashes it towards long-off. England taking wickets at regular intervals.

commentary_short: Axar to Buttler, Caught by Pant!! Big wicket for India. Length delivery on off-stump, just grips in the surface and turn
commentary: Axar to Buttler, Caught by Pant!! Big wicket for India. Length delivery on off-stump, just grips in the surface and turns, not characteristic for Axar as he usually skids it through, Buttler went for a reverse-sweep straightaway and it lobbed up after taking the toe end, Pant only had to turn around and take a step or two before completing an easy catch. Superb bowling change and it yields results instantly.

commentary_short: Bumrah to Philip Salt, Bowled!! Both openers are gone now and this is a Bumrah special! A lovely off cutter on off-stump
commentary: Bumrah to Philip Salt, Bowled!! Both openers are gone now and this is a Bumrah special! A lovely off cutter on off-stump, Philip Salt was not expecting the slower one this time, backed away a little and swiped across the line far too early, the ball cut towards leg-stump and hit the top of it. Bumrah is elated and so is the Indian team that surrounds him in celebration. What was looking like a good start for England has suddenly turned into a fantastic start for India.

commentary: Axar to Bairstow, Bowled!! If it was grip and turn that got the last wicket, this is Axar's classic skidder that's got this one! Full and just outside off, Bairstow didn't get his feet close to this, fished at it with his hands and played for turn, it landed just outside off and just went on with the arm to hit off-stump. Axar is absolutely delighted! He's got two wickets in two overs now and England are in some trouble. Losing three wickets in the powerplay seldom bodes well.

commentary: Axar to Moeen Ali, Stumped!! Axar is having an absolute field day out there with the ball! Length delivery on leg-stump, Moeen hung back looking to work it into the leg-side and it just brushed his pad and dribbled towards Pant, Moeen wasn't quite sure where that had gone and took a step down off-balance, that was enough for Pant to pick it up from next to the stumps and demolish leg-stump. That's the end of Moeen and England are in serious trouble.

commentary: Kuldeep Yadav to Sam Curran, Lbw!! England's fifth wicket goes down along with a review. A length delivery on off-stump, this was the wrong-un and Sam Curran had no clue, he went right back looking to work it away and the bat never got close to the ball, he was pinned right in front and umpire Tucker had no hesitation in raising the finger. Curran probably reviewed in hope of a miracle more than anything else because he was on his way after taking one look at the replay. India are absolutely bossing things here.

commentary_short: Maharaj to Rohit, Caught by Klaasen!! Indian lose an early wicket and it's the in-form Rohit Sharma! This is exactly wha
commentary: Maharaj to Rohit, Caught by Klaasen!! Indian lose an early wicket and it's the in-form Rohit Sharma! This is exactly what South Africa would've wanted! Tossed up full on off-stump, Rohit leant across and swept it hard, timed it well but did not keep it down and it went straight to Klaasen at backward square leg. He moved to his left and took it fairly low, good catch and South Africa are absolutely delighted. Things have gone to plan.

commentary_short: Maharaj to Pant, Caught by de Kock!! Pant had a sheepish look on his face as soon as South Africa went up in appeal and
commentary: Maharaj to Pant, Caught by de Kock!! Pant had a sheepish look on his face as soon as South Africa went up in appeal and he's gone for a duck! Dream start for South Africa, they've got rid of Pant as well. This was floated up full just outside off, Pant leant in looking to sweep and ended up taking it on the full, it hit the cue end of the bat and lobbed up, simple catch for QDK as it looped over him and landed just behind the stumps. The sweep has led to the downfall of both Rohit and Pant now.

commentary: Rabada to Suryakumar Yadav, Caught by Klaasen!! South Africa are absolutely pumped as they've picked up a third wicket in the powerplay! This is a sensational start now as SKY makes his way back to the pavilion as well. A length delivery on off-stump, Suryakumar Yadav shuffled across a touch and picked it up before pulling it away towards deep backward square leg, didn't look like he put everything into it, it was hit into the wind and it floated down to Klaasen who was there, he ran to his left and tumbled before taking it low. Another great catch and South Africa know the value of that wicket.

commentary_short: Rabada to Kohli, Axar Run Out!! Direct hit at the non-striker's end and South Africa are celebrating. What was Axar doin
commentary: Rabada to Kohli, Axar Run Out!! Direct hit at the non-striker's end and South Africa are celebrating. What was Axar doing there? Sloppy running from him and he's a few inches short of the ground. Back of a length delivery down leg, Kohli misses and it goes off his thigh pad to the left of de Kock. Axar comes down looking for a single when it's clearly not on, but he does have the time to make it back home if he charges back immediately. However, he doesn't expect the throw to come at his end and is slow to turn around. De Kock catches him by surprise. A superb knock ends in clumsy fashion.

commentary_short: Bumrah to Reeza Hendricks, Bowled!! ! The coaches and experts often keep telling batters about Bumrah's cunning angle an
commentary: Bumrah to Reeza Hendricks, Bowled!! ! The coaches and experts often keep telling batters about Bumrah's cunning angle and how it's tough to play him with that wide angle. But he's bowling some lovely genuine outswingers too to make life hell for batters and this is an unplayable ball. I was definitely not expecting it to swing from Bumrah kind of reaction to that ball from Reeza. Length and moving away very late, squaring up the batter and hitting top of off. One of the most satisfying feelings for a pacer, more so if you make the batter look clueless.

commentary: Arshdeep Singh to de Kock, , top shot!! The shortest part of the ground and that's the area Nasser Hussain mentioned at the pitch report that de Kock would target. A length ball angling into the batter, de Kock shuffles in and pulls it from his thighs over short fine for a flat six

commentary_short: Arshdeep Singh to de Kock, Caught by Kuldeep Yadav!! Same ball, different result!! A length ball angling onto the pads,
commentary: Arshdeep Singh to de Kock, Caught by Kuldeep Yadav!! Same ball, different result!! A length ball angling onto the pads, de Kock tried to pull it behind square but Kuldeep from short fine was pushed to the boundary. Straight down his throat and India get a timely breakthrough. T Dileep who was standing behind Kuldeep celebrates the most animatedly, everyone recognises the value of this wicket.

commentary: Bumrah to Marco Jansen, Bowled!!I0$ Because that right hand of this esteemed pacer is one of the most valuable things for Indian cricket. A superb inswinger, nips in after pitching on off stump and gets Jansen on the inside edge. The batter tried a simple defence. nothing extravagant and was beaten all ends up. Knocks the top of leg stump and Jasprit Bumrah celebrates. There is life in this game, the Indians voices are getting loud again.

commentary_short: Hardik Pandya to Miller, Caught by Suryakumar Yadav!! .. A low wide full toss from Hardik, Miller gets in line and heave
commentary: Hardik Pandya to Miller, Caught by Suryakumar Yadav!! .. A low wide full toss from Hardik, Miller gets in line and heaves it through the line. Gets enough power behind it although it was off the toe-end. Flies towards long-off and was going over but Surya appears out of nowhere to pluck it out as he moves to his left, and then juggle it up as he was going over the ropes. Had the feet millimetres away from the cushions too, did well not to step on it, lobs it back in as he goes out, comes back in, steadies himself and completes the catch.
